biteOfDay: Mid-summer rhythm—hit topwater for Table Rock bass at first light, then time your Taneycomo trout trip to the light overnight/early-morning flows before generation ramps back up in the afternoon!

tableRock.temp: Verify locally (typically low–mid 80s°F; guide-report sources unreachable this morning)
tableRock.level: 916.98 ft MSL (at full power pool of 917.0 ft — verified USACE gage 4 AM CT)

taneycomo.generation: Light overnight/early-morning flow (~3,400 cfs at 4 AM 07-20), ramping to heavy afternoon/evening generation (~11,600 cfs). Fish the low-flow morning window before the afternoon ramp-up.

species.name: Black Bass (Largemouth, Smallmouth, Spotted)
species.depth: Dawn points/gravel banks; mid-day 15–35 ft brush
species.technique: Topwater at dawn (Pop-R/walking bait); deep spoon, shakey head, deep crankbait mid-day

species.name: Walleye
species.technique: Crankbaits, live bait (carried from 07-14 report — not re-scouted today)

species.name: Crappie
species.depth: Deep timber & brush piles (James River/Long Creek arms)
species.technique: Small white or yellow jigs under deep-water docks

species.name: Trout (Taneycomo)
species.technique: Pink worms, mega worms, jigs, zebra midges, scuds under float; drift nightcrawlers in Trophy Area (Fall Creek–Cooper Creek)

species.name: Bluegill
species.technique: Worms, small poppers — fantastic for kids (carried from 07-14 report)

species.name: Catfish (Channel/Flathead)
species.depth: Indian Point & near dam, shallows after dark
species.technique: Stink bait, hot dogs, cut/frozen bait — evening/night

weather.high: Verify locally (local forecast not pulled this run)

tip: Fish early mornings. Table Rock topwater at sunrise. On Taneycomo, fish the low-flow morning window before the afternoon generation ramp-up; when generation kicks on, switch to drifting — trout turn on when water moves. Confirm generation by calling SWPA at 866-494-1993.
